expectations.text: contains-mode-label — Response includes 'Mode: Incident Analysis' or 'Incident Analysis' as a heading
expectations.evidence: The response title is 'Incident Analysis: EF Core Connection Pool Exhaustion' — while 'Incident Analysis' appears in the title, it is not labeled as 'Mode: Incident Analysis' and does not follow the expected mode-dispatch format.

expectations.text: contains-bote-pool-math — Response contains BotE pool math with numbers (connections x hold_time vs pool_size)
expectations.evidence: The response describes pool exhaustion conceptually but provides no explicit BotE calculation with numbers such as connections x hold_time vs pool_size. It says pool size is 100 but does not show the arithmetic.

expectations.text: identifies-root-cause — Response names a specific root cause
expectations.evidence: The response names 'Leaked DbContext / Unclosed Connections (Most Common)' as the top-ranked specific root cause, along with four additional specific causes.

expectations.text: includes-ef-core-fix — Response gives concrete EF Core-specific fix
expectations.evidence: The response provides concrete EF Core fixes including 'Use DI scoped lifetime', 'Change to AddDbContext<T>()', 'Use IDbContextFactory<T>', and 'Make entire call chain async', with code examples.

expectations.text: mentions-kos-capture — Response mentions generating KOS records
expectations.evidence: The response contains no mention of KOS records, knowledge capture, or any structured incident logging system.
